title: Define Business Problem
description: Clearly articulate the business problem or opportunity being addressed by this initiative. Ensure the problem statement is grounded in evidence, stakeholder input, and measurable pain points rather than pre-assumed solutions.
inputsNeeded: Current state pain points and friction data
expectedOutputs: Validated problem statement document
Opportunity brief with evidence and impact estimate

title: Define Project Goals
description: Establish clear, measurable project goals aligned with organizational strategy and executive expectations. Goals should be outcome-focused and serve as the north star for all subsequent planning and delivery decisions.
expectedOutputs: Project goals and objectives statement
Success criteria framework with measurable outcomes

title: Identify Stakeholders
description: Identify all individuals, groups, and organizations who have an interest in or influence over the initiative. Map stakeholder roles, influence levels, communication needs, and potential risks from disengagement or misalignment.
inputsNeeded: Prior project stakeholder lists if available
Executive sponsor input on key parties
expectedOutputs: Stakeholder register with roles and influence ratings
Initial engagement and communication approach per stakeholder group

title: Create Project Charter
description: Draft and obtain approval for the project charter, which formally authorizes the project and documents the scope, objectives, key milestones, budget, risks, and governance structure. The charter is the foundational contract between the project team and sponsoring organization.
inputsNeeded: Approved project goals and business problem statement
expectedOutputs: Signed project charter document
Summary one-pager for stakeholder distribution

title: Define Governance Cadence
description: Establish the recurring governance and reporting cadence for the initiative, including steering committee meetings, status reports, escalation paths, and decision-making protocols. A clear cadence ensures accountability and timely decision-making throughout the project lifecycle.
expectedOutputs: Governance calendar with recurring meeting schedule
Escalation matrix and decision-making framework

title: Create Communication Plan
description: Develop a structured communication plan that details what information is communicated, to whom, by whom, how frequently, and through which channels. A robust communication plan reduces misalignment, manages expectations, and builds stakeholder confidence.
expectedOutputs: Communication plan document with matrix of audience, message, channel, and frequency
Communication templates for key recurring updates

title: Create Initial RAID Log
description: Establish the initial RAID log (Risks, Assumptions, Issues, Dependencies) to capture and track known uncertainties and constraints from project inception. Proactive RAID management reduces surprises and enables early mitigation of threats to delivery.
inputsNeeded: Prior project lessons learned if available
expectedOutputs: Populated RAID log with initial entries and owners
Risk rating summary with top 5 risks and mitigation actions

title: Define Success Metrics
description: Define the specific, measurable metrics that will determine whether the project or product has delivered its intended value. Success metrics should be agreed with stakeholders upfront and linked directly to business outcomes, not just delivery outputs.
inputsNeeded: Project goals and business problem statement
expectedOutputs: Success metrics framework with baseline, target, and measurement method
KPI dashboard specification for ongoing tracking

title: Define AI Usage Guardrails
description: Establish clear policies and guardrails for how AI tools may be used by the team throughout the initiative. This includes defining approved use cases, prohibited data types, review requirements, and quality assurance expectations for AI-generated content.
inputsNeeded: Organizational AI policy and acceptable use standards
Client or regulatory requirements regarding AI
Team AI tooling and access inventory
expectedOutputs: AI usage policy document for the initiative
Approved AI tool register with use case guidance

title: Define Delivery Operating Model
description: Define how the delivery team will operate day-to-day, including team structure, roles and responsibilities, delivery methodology, tooling, meeting rhythms, and handoff protocols. A clear operating model ensures every team member understands expectations and how work flows from idea to delivery.
inputsNeeded: Team roster with roles and availability
Organizational delivery standards and methodology preferences
expectedOutputs: Delivery operating model document
Team working agreement and RACI matrix

title: Capture Stakeholder Notes
description: Systematically record and synthesize notes from all stakeholder interviews and discovery conversations, ensuring key insights, pain points, requirements signals, and open questions are captured in a structured and searchable format.
inputsNeeded: Recording or notes from discovery sessions
expectedOutputs: Structured stakeholder interview notes document
Key themes and insights synthesis summary

title: Build Domain Knowledge File
description: Create a consolidated domain knowledge reference file that captures the core concepts, terminology, business context, key entities, and domain rules relevant to the initiative. This file serves as the shared knowledge foundation for the delivery team and reduces onboarding time for new members.
expectedOutputs: Domain knowledge file with glossary and key concepts
Business entity and relationship summary

title: Map Business Workflows
description: Document the current business workflows and processes that the initiative will impact or improve. Use structured process notation to capture the steps, decision points, roles, and systems involved, enabling the team to identify inefficiencies and design better future-state processes.
inputsNeeded: Stakeholder interview notes and domain knowledge file
Access to existing process documentation if available
System access to observe current workflows
expectedOutputs: Current-state business workflow diagrams
Process narrative with pain points annotated

title: Identify User Personas
description: Develop data-informed user personas that represent the distinct groups of people who will interact with the product or system. Personas capture goals, behaviors, frustrations, and context to guide product decisions and ensure the team builds for real users, not abstract ones.
inputsNeeded: User research data or customer feedback
Analytics data on current system usage if available
Business workflows and current-state process maps
expectedOutputs: User persona profiles (3-6 key personas)
Persona-to-workflow mapping showing interaction points

title: Define Current-State Process
description: Create a comprehensive, validated documentation of the current-state processes, including all steps, exceptions, roles, systems, handoffs, and pain points. This artifact establishes the baseline from which future-state improvements will be measured.
expectedOutputs: Current-state process documentation with exception flows
Pain point and inefficiency register

title: Define Future-State Process
description: Design and document the desired future-state processes that the initiative will enable, showing how the business will operate once the solution is in place. Future-state maps should address identified pain points and align to agreed success metrics.
expectedOutputs: Future-state process diagrams with role assignments
Gap analysis between current and future state

title: Capture Business Rules
description: Elicit, document, and validate the business rules, policies, and constraints that the solution must enforce. Business rules govern how data is processed, decisions are made, and exceptions are handled within the system.
expectedOutputs: Business rules register with source and validation status
Decision table for complex conditional logic

title: Identify Open Questions
description: Systematically capture and track all open questions, assumptions requiring validation, and areas of ambiguity surfaced during discovery. Maintaining a visible open questions log prevents teams from making risky assumptions and drives structured follow-up with stakeholders.
inputsNeeded: Discovery session notes and interview outputs
expectedOutputs: Open questions log with owner and target resolution date
Prioritized list of questions blocking planning or design

title: Create Requirements Clarification Log
description: Maintain a structured log of all requirements clarifications, changes, and decisions made during discovery and analysis. This log provides an auditable trail that helps prevent scope disputes and ensures all team members work from the same validated understanding.
expectedOutputs: Requirements clarification log with decision rationale
Updated requirements document reflecting clarifications

title: Create Discovery Summary
description: Produce a consolidated discovery summary report that synthesizes all findings from the discovery phase into a coherent narrative. The summary should communicate the validated problem, key insights, user needs, business rules, and recommended direction to stakeholders in a clear and actionable format.
inputsNeeded: All discovery phase outputs (interviews, workflows, personas, business rules)
Open questions log with resolution status
expectedOutputs: Discovery summary report with executive summary
Recommended solution direction and next steps

title: Create Product Roadmap
description: Develop a strategic product roadmap that translates the product vision and discovery insights into a sequenced, outcome-based plan for delivery. The roadmap communicates what will be built and when, serving as the primary alignment tool between strategy and execution.
expectedOutputs: Product roadmap (now/next/later or quarterly format)
Roadmap narrative explaining prioritization rationale

title: Create Project Plan
description: Develop a detailed project plan including milestones, workstreams, task dependencies, resource assignments, and critical path. The project plan serves as the operational blueprint for delivering against the approved scope and timeline commitments.
expectedOutputs: Detailed project plan with milestone schedule
Critical path analysis and resource allocation matrix

title: Create Sprint Plan
description: Facilitate sprint planning to select backlog items for the upcoming sprint, establish the sprint goal, confirm team capacity, identify dependencies, and ensure all selected stories meet the definition of ready before development begins.
inputsNeeded: Team velocity data from prior sprints
Team capacity and leave schedule for the sprint
expectedOutputs: Sprint backlog with committed stories and sprint goal
Sprint planning record with capacity confirmation

title: Create Release Plan
description: Develop a release plan that maps features and capabilities to specific release dates or sprints, establishing a predictable delivery cadence. The release plan communicates what customers and stakeholders can expect and when, enabling proactive go-to-market and training preparation.
inputsNeeded: Dependency map across teams and systems
expectedOutputs: Release plan with features, dates, and milestones
Release communication schedule for stakeholders

title: Create Backlog Structure
description: Design and implement the backlog hierarchy and organization structure, establishing epics, features, and story groupings that reflect the product architecture and delivery strategy. A well-structured backlog enables efficient refinement, prioritization, and reporting.
expectedOutputs: Backlog structure with epic and feature hierarchy
Backlog organization conventions and tagging guide

title: Write Epics
description: Draft high-level epics that describe major bodies of work, capturing the business value, target persona, and scope boundaries for each theme. Well-written epics provide context that helps the team understand how individual stories contribute to larger outcomes.
expectedOutputs: Epic definitions with value statements and acceptance criteria
Epic-to-roadmap traceability mapping

title: Write User Stories
description: Decompose epics into well-formed user stories that describe specific user needs from the perspective of identified personas. User stories should be independently deliverable, negotiable, valuable, estimable, small, and testable (INVEST criteria).
expectedOutputs: User story library with persona, need, and value statement
Story readiness assessment against INVEST criteria

title: Write Acceptance Criteria
description: Define clear, testable acceptance criteria for each user story that specify the conditions the solution must meet to be accepted by the product owner. Acceptance criteria eliminate ambiguity, enable test case creation, and ensure shared understanding between product and engineering.
inputsNeeded: User stories with persona and need defined
Edge cases and exception flows from discovery
expectedOutputs: Acceptance criteria written in Given/When/Then or checklist format
Edge case and negative scenario coverage per story

title: Prioritize Backlog
description: Apply a structured prioritization framework to rank the product backlog by value, risk, and dependency, ensuring the team always works on the highest-impact items next. Prioritization decisions should be transparent, documented, and aligned with stakeholder expectations.
inputsNeeded: User stories and epics with effort estimates
expectedOutputs: Prioritized backlog with WSJF, MoSCoW, or equivalent scoring
Prioritization rationale document for stakeholder communication

title: Create Dependency Plan
description: Map and document all inbound and outbound dependencies between teams, systems, and third parties. A dependency plan enables proactive coordination, prevents delivery blockage, and ensures cross-team commitments are tracked and honored.
inputsNeeded: Backlog with technical stories and integration items
expectedOutputs: Dependency map with owners and resolution timelines
Dependency risk register with mitigation actions

title: Create Capacity Plan
description: Develop a team capacity plan that maps available engineering and delivery capacity to planned sprint and release commitments, accounting for leave, part-time allocations, and ramp-up time. Capacity planning prevents overcommitment and enables realistic forecasting.
expectedOutputs: Team capacity plan by sprint with available story points
Capacity risk flags for sprints with reduced availability

title: Create Solution Overview
description: Produce a high-level solution overview document that describes the overall technical approach, key components, integration points, and architectural style chosen for the initiative. The solution overview provides a shared technical context for the delivery team and non-technical stakeholders.
expectedOutputs: Solution overview document with component diagram
Technology selection rationale summary

title: Create Architecture Decision Record
description: Document key architectural decisions as Architecture Decision Records (ADRs), capturing the context, options considered, decision made, and consequences. ADRs create an auditable rationale for technical choices that future team members can reference when requirements evolve.
expectedOutputs: ADR documents for each significant architectural decision
ADR index and status tracker

title: Review Technical Constraints
description: Systematically review and document technical constraints that will affect design and development choices, including infrastructure limitations, legacy system dependencies, compliance requirements, budget constraints on tooling, and organizational technology standards.
expectedOutputs: Technical constraints register
Constraint impact analysis on proposed design approach

title: Define Integration Touchpoints
description: Identify and document all system integration points, APIs, data exchanges, and third-party service dependencies. Integration touchpoints represent significant technical risk and must be well-defined before development begins to prevent costly late-stage integration failures.
expectedOutputs: Integration touchpoint register with contract definitions
Integration sequence diagrams for key flows

title: Define Data Model Summary
description: Define the key data entities, attributes, relationships, and data flows for the solution. The data model summary guides database design, API contracts, and reporting requirements, ensuring all team members share a consistent understanding of the data domain.
inputsNeeded: Domain knowledge file and business rules
Non-functional requirements for data volume and retention
Existing data model documentation if available
expectedOutputs: Logical data model with entity relationship diagram
Data dictionary with key attributes and constraints

title: Create UX Flow
description: Design and document the user experience flows for key user journeys, showing the screens, interactions, and decision points that users will encounter. UX flows ensure the solution design centers on user goals and validates that all user stories are addressable within the proposed interaction model.
inputsNeeded: Wireframes or design prototypes if available
Business workflows and future-state process maps
expectedOutputs: UX flow diagrams for key user journeys
Interaction specification for complex screens

title: Create Process Flow
description: Develop detailed process flow diagrams for the solution's operational workflows, showing the step-by-step sequence of activities, decision branches, roles, and system interactions. Process flows bridge business requirements and technical implementation by making behavior explicit.
inputsNeeded: UX flows and user journey maps
expectedOutputs: Detailed process flow diagrams per business workflow
Exception and error handling flows

title: Define Non-Functional Requirements
description: Elicit, document, and validate non-functional requirements (NFRs) covering performance, scalability, availability, security, maintainability, and compliance. NFRs are often overlooked until late in delivery but have major architectural implications that must be addressed in design.
inputsNeeded: Expected user load and data volume projections
expectedOutputs: NFR specification with measurable acceptance thresholds
NFR-to-architecture traceability matrix

title: Review Security Considerations
description: Conduct a structured review of security considerations relevant to the solution design, including authentication, authorization, data protection, API security, and threat modeling. Security reviews at the design phase are far cheaper than remediating vulnerabilities discovered post-deployment.
expectedOutputs: Security review findings document with risk ratings
Security requirements backlog items for development

title: Create Design Review Checklist
description: Compile a comprehensive design review checklist that validates all design deliverables meet required standards before development begins. The checklist should cover architectural completeness, NFR coverage, security review, integration definitions, and team understanding.
expectedOutputs: Design review checklist with completion status
Design phase sign-off record with outstanding items

title: Create Implementation Plan
description: Develop a detailed engineering implementation plan that sequences technical work, assigns ownership, identifies integration milestones, and establishes checkpoints for quality validation. The implementation plan translates architecture and design into an actionable engineering execution roadmap.
expectedOutputs: Engineering implementation plan with sequenced workstreams
Team assignment matrix and sprint allocation

title: Break Stories into Technical Tasks
description: Decompose user stories into concrete technical tasks that individual engineers can complete within a day or two, enabling accurate tracking, parallel development, and early detection of implementation blockers. Technical task breakdown is a key enabler of sprint predictability.
inputsNeeded: Sprint backlog with ready user stories
Engineering team skills and familiarity with the domain
expectedOutputs: Technical task breakdown per story in the sprint backlog
Story-to-task dependency map for parallel development

title: Define Coding Standards
description: Establish and document the coding standards, style guides, and engineering conventions the team will follow during development. Consistent coding standards reduce code review friction, improve maintainability, and enable engineers to work confidently in each other's code.
inputsNeeded: Linting, formatting, and static analysis tooling
expectedOutputs: Coding standards document per language or framework
Linter and formatter configuration committed to repository

title: Create Code Review Checklist
description: Define a code review checklist that guides reviewers to assess submissions consistently against quality, security, performance, and maintainability criteria. A structured checklist elevates the quality of code reviews from subjective style debates to objective engineering standards.
inputsNeeded: Non-functional requirements for performance and security
Security review findings from design phase
expectedOutputs: Code review checklist published to team wiki or repository
Code review process and SLA document

title: Track Technical Risks
description: Continuously identify, assess, and track technical risks that emerge during development, including integration uncertainties, performance concerns, technical debt accumulation, and dependency delays. Active technical risk management prevents late-stage surprises and enables proactive mitigation.
inputsNeeded: Engineering team input and standup blockers
expectedOutputs: Updated technical risk register with mitigation actions
Risk status summary for inclusion in project status report

title: Track Blockers
description: Maintain a visible blocker log and ensure every impediment affecting team progress is owned, escalated appropriately, and resolved as quickly as possible. Unresolved blockers are the most common cause of sprint failure and must be treated with urgency and transparency.
inputsNeeded: Team communication channels and flagged items
expectedOutputs: Active blocker log with owner and resolution status
Escalation record for blockers requiring leadership intervention

title: Prepare Developer Handoff
description: Create structured developer handoff documentation that provides engineers with the context, design specifications, acceptance criteria, and technical guidance needed to implement a story correctly without constant interruption to the BA or architect. Good handoffs reduce clarification loops and improve development quality.
expectedOutputs: Developer handoff notes per story or epic
Technical implementation guidance with examples

title: Create Branch/Release Strategy
description: Define the version control branching strategy and release tagging conventions the team will follow, ensuring consistent, conflict-free collaboration across engineers and predictable release packaging. A clear branching strategy is foundational to a safe CI/CD pipeline.
inputsNeeded: Team size and parallel workstream count
expectedOutputs: Branching strategy document with naming conventions
Release tagging and versioning guide

title: Create Local Development Notes
description: Document the steps required to set up and run the solution in a local development environment, including dependencies, configuration, tooling, and common troubleshooting tips. Good local development documentation dramatically reduces onboarding time and resolves environment issues faster.
expectedOutputs: Local development setup guide published to repository README
Troubleshooting guide for common local environment issues

title: Create Engineering Execution Summary
description: Produce a regular engineering execution summary that reports on development progress, technical health, key decisions made, blockers resolved, and risks on the horizon. This summary keeps leadership and delivery management informed without requiring constant interruption of the engineering team.
inputsNeeded: Code quality and test coverage metrics
expectedOutputs: Engineering execution summary report
Technical health dashboard update

title: Create Test Strategy
description: Define the overall testing strategy for the initiative, covering the types of testing to be performed, testing levels, entry and exit criteria, test environment requirements, roles and responsibilities, and the tools to be used. A solid test strategy aligns the team on quality expectations before testing begins.
expectedOutputs: Test strategy document approved by key stakeholders
Testing scope and out-of-scope boundaries

title: Create Test Plan
description: Develop a detailed test plan that operationalizes the test strategy, specifying test phases, test cycle schedules, resource assignments, environment requirements, and defect management processes. The test plan is the tactical execution guide for the testing phase.
expectedOutputs: Detailed test plan with test cycle schedule
Resource and environment allocation plan for testing

title: Create Test Scenarios
description: Develop high-level test scenarios that describe the conditions and workflows to be tested, covering happy paths, edge cases, exception flows, and integration scenarios. Test scenarios serve as the bridge between business requirements and detailed test cases.
expectedOutputs: Test scenario library mapped to user stories and epics
Coverage map showing requirements-to-scenario traceability

title: Create Test Cases
description: Write detailed, step-by-step test cases from test scenarios, specifying preconditions, test data, execution steps, and expected results. Detailed test cases enable consistent test execution across testers and provide an audit trail of quality validation.
inputsNeeded: Business rules and edge case documentation
Test data requirements and data sets
expectedOutputs: Detailed test case library in test management tool
Test data specification and setup instructions

title: Define UAT Readiness Checklist
description: Define the criteria and checklist that must be satisfied before User Acceptance Testing begins, ensuring the solution is stable, environments are ready, test data is in place, and business users are prepared to participate. UAT readiness gates prevent wasted business stakeholder time on unstable builds.
inputsNeeded: Definition of Done for development stories
expectedOutputs: UAT readiness checklist with go/no-go criteria
UAT kick-off agenda and participant briefing pack

title: Define Regression Scope
description: Define the scope of regression testing for each release, identifying which existing features and functionality must be re-validated to ensure new changes have not introduced unintended breakage. Regression scope definition balances thoroughness with testing time constraints.
inputsNeeded: Feature inventory and change impact analysis
Prior defect history and high-risk areas
expectedOutputs: Regression test scope document with prioritized test suites
Risk-based regression coverage rationale

title: Define Automation Scope
description: Define which test scenarios and test cases are candidates for test automation, considering return on investment, test frequency, stability, and technical feasibility. A pragmatic automation scope accelerates regression coverage while avoiding over-engineering on tests that change frequently.
inputsNeeded: Test case library with execution frequency
expectedOutputs: Test automation scope and prioritized automation backlog
Automation ROI assessment and tooling decision

title: Track Defect Leakage
description: Monitor and report on defect leakage metrics, tracking how many defects are discovered in testing versus in production, and by which testing phase defects are caught. Defect leakage data informs improvements to test coverage and quality gates.
expectedOutputs: Defect leakage report by phase and severity
Root cause analysis for critical defect escapes

title: Create Test Summary
description: Produce a test summary report at the end of each testing phase that documents what was tested, the results achieved, open defects, test coverage metrics, and a recommendation on release readiness. The test summary provides stakeholders with the quality evidence needed to make go/no-go decisions.
inputsNeeded: Test execution results from test management tool
expectedOutputs: Test summary report with release readiness recommendation
Outstanding defect risk assessment for release decision

title: Create Quality Risk Assessment
description: Assess the quality risks associated with releasing the current build, considering open defects, test coverage gaps, untested integration points, and environmental differences from production. The quality risk assessment provides decision-makers with a clear view of release risk exposure.
expectedOutputs: Quality risk assessment with risk-rated go/no-go recommendation
Risk acceptance sign-off from stakeholders for known gaps

title: Define Deployment Plan
description: Create a detailed deployment plan that specifies the sequence of deployment steps, environment order, configuration changes, data migrations, downtime requirements, and validation checkpoints. A well-documented deployment plan reduces execution risk and enables confident, repeatable releases.
expectedOutputs: Step-by-step deployment plan with timing and owners
Pre-deployment and post-deployment validation checklist

title: Create Release Checklist
description: Compile a comprehensive release checklist covering all activities and gates that must be completed before a release is authorized to proceed to production. The checklist spans technical, business, communication, and operational readiness dimensions.
expectedOutputs: Release checklist with completion status and owner per item
Release go/no-go decision record

title: Define Rollback Plan
description: Design and document the rollback procedure to revert the production system to a known-good state in the event of a failed or problematic deployment. A tested rollback plan is essential insurance for any production release and must be validated before go-live.
inputsNeeded: Database migration scripts and reversal procedures
Rollback trigger criteria and decision authority
expectedOutputs: Rollback procedure document with step-by-step instructions
Rollback trigger criteria and decision matrix

title: Create Environment Readiness Checklist
description: Validate that all target deployment environments are ready to receive the release, including infrastructure provisioning, configuration validation, access credentials, monitoring setup, and dependency service availability. Environment issues at deployment time are among the most common causes of release failures.
inputsNeeded: Infrastructure as code and environment scripts
expectedOutputs: Environment readiness checklist with pass/fail status
Open environment issues with resolution owner and deadline

title: Define Pipeline Readiness
description: Validate that the CI/CD pipeline is configured, tested, and ready to support the production deployment, including all build stages, automated test gates, deployment scripts, and artifact management. Pipeline readiness ensures the deployment process is automated, auditable, and repeatable.
inputsNeeded: Pipeline test results from staging deployments
Artifact repository and version management setup
expectedOutputs: Pipeline readiness assessment with validated stages
Pipeline test execution evidence from pre-production runs

title: Define Monitoring Requirements
description: Define the monitoring, alerting, and observability requirements for the production system, ensuring the team has sufficient visibility into system health, performance, errors, and business metrics post-deployment. Effective monitoring enables rapid incident detection and response.
inputsNeeded: Non-functional requirements for performance and availability
expectedOutputs: Monitoring requirements document with alert thresholds
Observability implementation backlog items

title: Create Deployment Communication
description: Prepare and coordinate deployment communications to inform all affected stakeholders about the upcoming release, including timing, expected impact, maintenance windows, and what to do if issues are encountered. Proactive communication reduces user confusion and support ticket volume during releases.
expectedOutputs: Deployment communication drafts for all audiences
Communication send schedule tied to deployment timeline

title: Create Smoke Test Checklist
description: Define a rapid smoke test checklist to be executed immediately after deployment to verify that the system's core functions are operational. Smoke tests provide fast confidence that the deployment was successful before undertaking full regression or opening to users.
inputsNeeded: Integration touchpoints and system health indicators
expectedOutputs: Smoke test checklist with expected results per step
Smoke test execution record template

title: Create Post-Deployment Validation
description: Execute and document post-deployment validation activities to confirm the production environment is performing as expected after the release, including health checks, monitoring review, user acceptance spot checks, and business metric validation.
expectedOutputs: Post-deployment validation report
Go-live confirmation sign-off record

title: Create Change Approval Summary
description: Prepare the change approval documentation required by the Change Advisory Board (CAB) or governance body, summarizing the scope of change, testing evidence, deployment approach, rollback plan, and risk assessment. Complete change approval documentation enables timely release authorization.
inputsNeeded: Test summary and quality risk assessment
expectedOutputs: Change approval request document for CAB submission
CAB decision record with approval or conditions

title: Prepare Sprint Demo
description: Plan and facilitate the sprint review demonstration to showcase completed sprint work to stakeholders, gather feedback, and validate that delivered functionality meets business expectations. An effective sprint demo drives stakeholder engagement and creates momentum for the product.
inputsNeeded: Completed sprint backlog with accepted stories
expectedOutputs: Sprint demo agenda and script
Sprint review feedback log and action items

title: Prepare Release Notes
description: Author clear, user-friendly release notes that communicate what has changed, what is new, what has been fixed, and any known limitations in the release. Good release notes reduce support volume, set accurate expectations, and build customer confidence in the delivery team.
inputsNeeded: Completed user stories and acceptance criteria
expectedOutputs: Customer-facing release notes document
Internal technical change log

title: Create Customer Status Report
description: Produce a clear and professional customer-facing status report summarizing delivery progress, milestones achieved, upcoming deliverables, risks, and issues. Customer status reports maintain trust, manage expectations, and demonstrate delivery accountability to client stakeholders.
inputsNeeded: Sprint review outputs and velocity data
expectedOutputs: Customer status report in agreed format
Status report distribution record

title: Create Executive Summary
description: Produce a concise executive summary that communicates program or project status to senior leadership in a format optimized for quick consumption. Executive summaries must distill complex delivery realities into clear headlines, key decisions needed, and risk indicators.
inputsNeeded: Project status reports and milestone data
expectedOutputs: Executive summary deck or document (one page preferred)
Key decision points and escalation items highlighted

title: Create Stakeholder Update
description: Prepare and distribute a regular stakeholder update that keeps all relevant parties informed of progress, decisions, changes, and upcoming activities. Consistent stakeholder updates prevent information vacuums that breed anxiety and rumor.
inputsNeeded: Sprint review outputs and velocity metrics
Open risks and issues from RAID log
expectedOutputs: Stakeholder update communication in agreed format
Distribution confirmation and engagement tracking

title: Track Customer Decisions
description: Maintain a structured log of all decisions made by the customer, including design choices, scope trade-offs, and risk acceptances. A decision log protects the delivery team from scope disputes and ensures customer-owned decisions are documented with appropriate sign-off.
expectedOutputs: Customer decision log with decision, owner, and date
Decision impact summary per affected workstream

title: Prepare Training Notes
description: Develop training materials and user guidance that enable end users and support staff to effectively use the new system or feature. Training notes should be tailored to each audience, covering key workflows, tips, and common questions.
inputsNeeded: Completed user stories and final UX flows
User personas and their common workflows
expectedOutputs: User training guide per key persona or role
Quick reference card for high-frequency workflows

title: Prepare Go-Live Checklist
description: Compile and execute the go-live checklist that confirms all technical, business, operational, and communication readiness requirements have been met before opening the system to production users. The go-live checklist is the final gate before customer impact.
expectedOutputs: Completed go-live checklist with sign-offs
Go-live authorization record

title: Create Adoption Plan
description: Develop a plan to drive user adoption of the new system or feature, including communication campaigns, training schedules, champion networks, and adoption measurement mechanisms. High adoption is the difference between a technically successful release and a business outcome.
inputsNeeded: User personas and adoption barriers from discovery
expectedOutputs: User adoption plan with activities and timeline
Adoption metrics dashboard specification

title: Capture Feedback
description: Systematically collect and synthesize feedback from customers, end users, and stakeholders following release, identifying satisfaction levels, usability issues, unmet needs, and opportunities for improvement. Structured feedback collection closes the loop between delivery and value realization.
inputsNeeded: User feedback survey or interview schedule
Support ticket data from post-release period
expectedOutputs: Feedback synthesis report with key themes
Improvement backlog items from user feedback

title: Create Runbook
description: Document operational runbooks that provide step-by-step instructions for common operational tasks, routine maintenance procedures, and known failure scenarios. Well-maintained runbooks reduce mean time to recovery (MTTR) and enable on-call engineers to resolve issues consistently.
inputsNeeded: Common failure modes identified during development
Prior incident records and resolution steps
expectedOutputs: Operational runbook library per system component
On-call quick reference guide

title: Create Support Model
description: Define the operational support model for the production system, including support tiers, escalation paths, SLA commitments per tier, support team responsibilities, and tooling. A documented support model ensures customers receive consistent, timely assistance and internal teams understand their obligations.
inputsNeeded: SLA and SLO definitions from the initiative
expectedOutputs: Support model document with tier definitions and SLAs
Support team responsibility matrix and escalation tree

title: Create Incident Response Plan
description: Develop a documented incident response plan that defines how the team detects, triages, responds to, communicates about, and learns from production incidents. An effective incident response plan reduces customer impact and drives continuous improvement in system reliability.
inputsNeeded: SLA breach thresholds and customer notification requirements
expectedOutputs: Incident response plan with roles and response steps
Incident communication templates for customer and internal use

title: Track Production Issues
description: Maintain a structured register of production issues, tracking their status, impact, priority, root cause, and resolution. Active production issue tracking ensures nothing falls through the cracks and provides data for trend analysis and preventive improvement.
inputsNeeded: Monitoring alerts and system health logs
expectedOutputs: Production issue register with current status
Weekly production health summary

title: Track Operational Metrics
description: Monitor, collect, and report on operational performance metrics including system availability, response time, error rates, throughput, and support volume trends. Operational metrics provide the data foundation for SLA compliance reporting, capacity planning, and continuous improvement prioritization.
inputsNeeded: Business KPIs tied to system performance
expectedOutputs: Operational metrics dashboard with trend analysis
Monthly SLA compliance report

title: Create SLA/SLO Summary
description: Produce a regular SLA and SLO summary report that documents performance against contractual and internal service level commitments, highlighting breaches, near-misses, trends, and improvement actions. The SLA summary is critical for client trust and internal quality governance.
inputsNeeded: SLA and SLO definitions and thresholds
expectedOutputs: SLA/SLO summary report with compliance percentages
Breach analysis and root cause summary

title: Create Monitoring Dashboard Requirements
description: Define the requirements for operational monitoring dashboards that give the team real-time visibility into system health, user experience, and business metrics. Dashboard requirements should balance technical detail for engineers with business-readable indicators for operational stakeholders.
inputsNeeded: Business KPIs linked to the system
expectedOutputs: Monitoring dashboard requirements specification
Dashboard design prototype or wireframe

title: Create Backup/Restore Notes
description: Document the backup and restore procedures for all production data stores and configuration, including backup schedules, retention policies, restore procedures, and testing cadence. Tested and documented backup procedures are foundational to business continuity.
inputsNeeded: Recovery time objective (RTO) and recovery point objective (RPO) requirements
expectedOutputs: Backup and restore procedure documentation
Backup test schedule and results record

title: Create Troubleshooting Guide
description: Develop a troubleshooting guide that helps engineers and support staff diagnose and resolve common issues efficiently. The guide should cover known failure patterns, diagnostic steps, system dependencies to check, and escalation criteria.
inputsNeeded: Production issue register and incident history
expectedOutputs: Troubleshooting guide organized by symptom or component
Diagnostic decision tree for complex issues

title: Create Continuous Improvement Backlog
description: Establish and maintain a continuous improvement backlog capturing operational enhancement opportunities, technical debt items, process improvements, and lessons from incidents and feedback. A curated improvement backlog ensures operational excellence compounds over time rather than stagnating.
inputsNeeded: Production issue trends and root cause analysis
Operational metrics and SLA breach history
expectedOutputs: Prioritized continuous improvement backlog
Quarterly improvement roadmap with business case

title: Create Project Closeout Report
description: Produce a comprehensive project closeout report that formally documents the project's outcomes, scope delivered versus planned, financial actuals versus budget, key decisions made, risks encountered, and satisfaction levels. The closeout report serves as the definitive project record and organizational learning artifact.
expectedOutputs: Project closeout report with executive summary
Final project dashboard and KPI summary

title: Capture Lessons Learned
description: Facilitate a structured lessons learned session and document key insights from the initiative, covering what went well, what did not go well, and what the team would do differently. Lessons learned must be captured in a searchable, reusable format to create genuine organizational learning.
inputsNeeded: Team retrospective outputs from all phases
RAID log history and closed items
expectedOutputs: Lessons learned register with categorized insights
Recommended process or practice changes for future initiatives
relatedPlaybooks: Lessons Learned Capture and Action Playbook

title: Create Retrospective Summary
description: Facilitate and document the final team retrospective, capturing honest reflections on the team's process, collaboration, and delivery practices across the full engagement. The retrospective summary should translate discussion into concrete, owned improvement actions.
inputsNeeded: Sprint retrospective records from all sprints
Team health metrics and happiness data
expectedOutputs: Final retrospective summary with themes and insights
Improvement action register with owners and timelines
relatedPlaybooks: Lessons Learned Capture and Action Playbook

title: Evaluate Success Metrics
description: Assess actual outcomes against the success metrics defined at project initiation, measuring whether the initiative delivered its intended business value. Success metric evaluation closes the outcome accountability loop and provides evidence for benefits realization reporting.
inputsNeeded: Business analytics and KPI data post-delivery
expectedOutputs: Success metrics evaluation report with actual versus target
Benefits realization assessment and outstanding opportunity

title: Capture Reusable Playbooks
description: Identify, document, and package process playbooks, templates, and delivery patterns from the initiative that have sufficient quality and generalizability to be reused on future projects. Reusable playbooks are among the highest-value knowledge assets an organization can build.
inputsNeeded: All delivery phase artifacts and templates
Team feedback on what tools and processes worked best
expectedOutputs: Set of refined, reusable playbook documents
Playbook library update with new additions and amendments
relatedPlaybooks: Lessons Learned Capture and Action Playbook

title: Update Skill Evidence
description: Document and capture concrete evidence of skills demonstrated during the initiative by team members, linking delivery artifacts to skill categories. Skill evidence records support career development, performance reviews, and capability building conversations.
inputsNeeded: Team performance observations throughout the initiative
Key artifacts authored by each team member
expectedOutputs: Skill evidence records per team member
Capability development recommendations per person

title: Archive Project Notes
description: Ensure all project documentation, decision records, correspondence, and artifacts are organized, labeled, and stored in a retrievable archive. Proper archival protects the organization's investment in project knowledge and ensures compliance with record retention requirements.
inputsNeeded: All project phase deliverables and documents
Decision log and change request history
expectedOutputs: Completed project archive with organized folder structure
Archive index document for easy navigation
relatedPlaybooks: Lessons Learned Capture and Action Playbook

title: Create Improvement Roadmap
description: Synthesize lessons learned, continuous improvement backlog items, and success metric gaps into a forward-looking improvement roadmap that prioritizes the most impactful changes for the team or product in the next cycle. The improvement roadmap turns retrospective insight into actionable future commitment.
inputsNeeded: Team capability assessment and development needs
expectedOutputs: Improvement roadmap with prioritized initiatives
Improvement initiative ownership and accountability plan

title: Create Reusable Prompt Packs
description: Document and package the most valuable AI prompts used during the initiative into structured, reusable prompt packs that can be shared with the broader organization. Prompt packs institutionalize AI-assisted delivery practices and accelerate knowledge transfer to future teams.
inputsNeeded: AI prompts used throughout the initiative
Prompt quality feedback from team members
AI usage guardrails and governance policy
expectedOutputs: Curated prompt pack library organized by phase and role
Prompt pack usage guide and governance notes
relatedPlaybooks: Lessons Learned Capture and Action Playbook

title: Export Knowledge Bundle
description: Compile and export a complete knowledge bundle from the initiative, packaging all reusable artifacts, playbooks, prompt packs, lessons learned, and domain knowledge files into a transferable format. The knowledge bundle preserves institutional memory and accelerates future initiatives in the same domain.
expectedOutputs: Complete knowledge bundle in a structured, portable format
Knowledge bundle handover and onboarding guide
relatedPlaybooks: Lessons Learned Capture and Action Playbook
